MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:
Alexandra Sologub, Esq. of WFBM LLP, 714.634.2522, for defendants General Electric Company and Paramount Global;
No appearance any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: Plaintiff Walter Baehr's Motion For Order Substituting Donna Baehr As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substituting Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff; And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Not contested, no argument is held. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Substituting Donna Baehr as Successor-in-Interest to, and Substituting Successor-in-Interest for Deceased Plaintiff; And For Leave to File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. (C.C.P. 377.20, 377.31, 377.11, 377.32, 377.60, and 473.) No opposition filed. Plaintiff shall file and serve the First Amended Complaint forthwith. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all Defendants who have previously appeared in the action.

Order signed.

Off Calendar Jul-27-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than August 10, 2023, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than August 10, 2023, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on September 28, 2023 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than October 6, 2023. Responses to the demand are due no later than October 20, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Pang Ly via electronic mail by November 10, 2023 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, November 16, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jul-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Mariam Pambukyan of WFBM, LLP (714-634-2522), appearing for defendants General Electric Company and Paramount Global.

RULING:
Not argued, not contested, and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed.

The trial date is November 20, 2023 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is November 27, 2023. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 12a, subd. (a); Code Civ. Proc., sec. 135.) Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is November 16, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is November 3, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is November 17, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than August 24, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)
